question: Is the image composed as a realistic overhead (top-down) view?
Does the image depict a museum display case environment?
Are there exactly six distinct recording media artifacts displayed in the case?
Are the artifacts arranged linearly from left to right in the correct historical order: Cuneiform Tablet -> Papyrus Scroll -> Medieval Codex -> Gutenberg Page -> Typewritten Doc -> E-reader?
Is the leftmost object a Sumerian cuneiform clay tablet with characteristic wedge-shaped impressions?
Is the second object an Egyptian papyrus scroll exhibiting fibrous texture and a rolled format?
Is the third object a medieval parchment manuscript presented in codex (bound book) format?
Is the fourth object an early printed page (Gutenberg era) showing evidence of movable type (e.g., uniform blackletter font)?
Is the fifth object a 20th-century typewritten document with monospaced characters and mechanical ink qualities?
Is the rightmost object a modern digital e-ink reader with a screen?
Is the e-ink reader depicted without any visible brand name or logo (unbranded)?
Are dates, era names, and explanatory text placards absent from the image?
Are all six artifacts fully visible within the frame, without being cut off by the image boundaries?
Does the image have a realistic photographic quality as specified in the prompt?
